# Kagan Documentation

> Documentation for Kagan: an open-source daemon that orchestrates AI coding agents with a terminal Kanban board, MCP server, and CLI.

Kagan runs 14+ AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and more) in parallel on isolated git worktrees. Every task is tracked from backlog to merge in a keyboard-first TUI.

## Docs

- [Home](https://docs.kagan.sh/): Documentation home
- [Quickstart](https://docs.kagan.sh/quickstart/): 60-second setup guide
- [Architecture Overview](https://docs.kagan.sh/concepts/architecture-overview/): System components and supported agents
- [Task Lifecycle](https://docs.kagan.sh/concepts/task-lifecycle/): How tasks flow from backlog to merge
- [Managed Runs](https://docs.kagan.sh/guides/managed-vs-interactive/): Run modes — managed vs interactive attach
- [Chat & REPL](https://docs.kagan.sh/guides/chat/): Interactive chat orchestrator
- [ACP Sessions](https://docs.kagan.sh/guides/acp-session-lifecycle/): Chat session lifecycle management
- [MCP Setup](https://docs.kagan.sh/guides/mcp-setup/): Editor integration via MCP
- [GitHub Import](https://docs.kagan.sh/guides/github/): Import issues from GitHub
- [GitHub Models](https://docs.kagan.sh/guides/github-models/): Use 40+ LLMs for free via GitHub Models
- [Remote Access](https://docs.kagan.sh/guides/remote-access/): Access Kagan from web or phone
- [CLI Reference](https://docs.kagan.sh/reference/cli/): All CLI commands and flags
- [Configuration](https://docs.kagan.sh/reference/configuration/): Config.toml reference
- [Keybindings](https://docs.kagan.sh/reference/keybindings/): TUI keyboard shortcuts
- [MCP Tools](https://docs.kagan.sh/reference/mcp-tools/): Full MCP tool catalog (23+ tools)
- [Plugins](https://docs.kagan.sh/reference/plugins/): Plugin system and SDK
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.kagan.sh/troubleshooting/): Common issues and fixes

## Links

- [Landing Page](https://kagan.sh/): Product homepage
- [GitHub](https://github.com/kagan-sh/kagan): Source code
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/kagan/): Python package
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/dB5AgMwWMy): Community
